title: ConfigFile
description:
Top-level Barnum configuration file format.

Defines a workflow as a directed graph of steps. Each step processes tasks and can spawn follow-up tasks on other steps.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema URL for editor validation (e.g., `"./node_modules/@barnum/barnum/barnum-config-schema.json"`). Ignored at runtime.

entrypoint.description: Name of the step that starts the workflow. When set, the CLI accepts `--entrypoint-value` to provide the initial task value (defaults to `{}`). When omitted, `--initial-state` must provide explicit `[{"kind": "StepName", "value": ...}]` tasks.

options.description: Global runtime options (timeout, retries, concurrency). Individual steps can override these via their own `options` field.

steps.description: The steps that make up this workflow. Each step defines how to process a task and which steps it can spawn follow-up tasks on.

ActionFile.description: How a step processes tasks. Set `"kind": "Pool"` to send tasks to AI agents, or `"kind": "Command"` to run a local shell script.

oneOf.description: Send the task to the agent pool for processing.

CommandActionFile.description: Run a local shell command instead of sending to an agent. Use this for deterministic transformations, fan-out, or glue logic.

script.description:
Shell script to execute.

**Input (stdin):** JSON object: `{"kind": "<step name>", "value": <payload>}`. Use `jq '.value'` to extract the payload, or `jq -r '.value.fieldName'` for a specific field.

**Output (stdout):** JSON array of follow-up tasks to spawn: `[{"kind": "NextStep", "value": {...}}, ...]`. Each `kind` must be a step name listed in this step's `next` array. Return `[]` to spawn no follow-ups.

FinallyHook.description:
Finally hook. Runs after a task and all its descendants complete.

In JSON: `{"kind": "Command", "params": {"script": "./finally-hook.sh"}}`

**stdin:** JSON object: `{"kind": "<step name>", "value": <payload>}`. **stdout:** JSON array of follow-up tasks: `[{"kind": "StepName", "value": {...}}, ...]`. Return `[]` for no follow-ups.

oneOf.description: Run a shell command as the finally hook.

HookCommand.description: A shell command used as a hook.

MaybeLinked_for_String.description:
Content that can be inline or linked to a file.

In config files: - `{"kind": "Inline", "value": <content>}` → content provided directly in the config - `{"kind": "Link", "path": "file.md"}` → content loaded from a file (path relative to the config file)

value.description: The content value, provided directly in the config file.

oneOf.description: Link to a file whose contents will be loaded at runtime.

path.description: Relative path to the file (resolved relative to the config file's directory).

Options.description: Global runtime options for task execution. All fields have sensible defaults.

retry_on_invalid_response.description: Whether to retry when agent returns invalid response (default: true).

retry_on_timeout.description: Whether to retry when agent times out (default: true).

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for each task (None = no timeout).

PoolActionFile.description: Send the task to the agent pool. An AI agent receives the task's `value` along with the `instructions` (markdown prompt) and produces a JSON array of follow-up tasks.

instructions.description: Markdown prompt shown to the agent processing this task. This is the core of what tells the agent what to do. Use `{"kind": "Inline", "value": "..."}` to write the markdown directly, or `{"kind": "Link", "path": "path/to/file.md"}` to reference an external file.

pool.description: Pool name (e.g., `"demo"`, `"reviewers"`). If omitted, the pool infrastructure uses its own default.

root.description: Pool root directory. If omitted, the pool infrastructure uses its own default.

timeout.description: Agent timeout in seconds. Passed to the pool as `timeout_seconds` in the task payload. Controls how long the agent gets to work. Separate from the step-level `timeout` which controls barnum's worker timeout.

StepFile.description:
A named step in the workflow. Steps are the nodes of the task graph.

The `finally` hook runs after the task **and all of its descendant tasks** complete.

action.description: How this step processes tasks — either send to the agent pool (`Pool`) or run a local shell command (`Command`).

finally.description:
Shell script that runs after this task **and all tasks it spawned (recursively)** have completed.

**stdin:** JSON object: `{"kind": "<step name>", "value": <payload>}`. Same envelope format as command action scripts.

**stdout:** A JSON array of follow-up tasks to spawn: `[{"kind": "StepName", "value": {...}}, ...]`. Each `kind` must be a valid step name. Return `[]` to spawn no follow-ups.

Use this for cleanup, aggregation, or spawning a final summarization step after an entire subtree of work completes.

name.description: Unique name for this step (e.g., `"Analyze"`, `"Implement"`, `"Review"`). This is the string used as `kind` when creating tasks: `{"kind": "ThisStepName", "value": {...}}`.

next.description: Step names this step is allowed to spawn follow-up tasks on. Each string must match the `name` of another step in this config. An empty array means this is a terminal step (no follow-ups).

options.description: Per-step options that override the global `options`. Only the fields you set here take effect; everything else falls through to the global defaults.

StepOptions.description: Per-step option overrides. Only set the fields you want to override; omitted fields inherit from the global `options`.

max_retries.description: Maximum retries for tasks on this step (overrides global `max_retries`).

retry_on_invalid_response.description: Whether to retry when an agent returns an invalid response on this step (overrides global `retry_on_invalid_response`).

retry_on_timeout.description: Whether to retry when an agent times out on this step (overrides global `retry_on_timeout`).

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for tasks on this step (overrides global `timeout`).
